data.description:
## Why
Keep the single-instance bot alive under hangs, controllable in an incident, and ‚Äî critically ‚Äî **crash-safe**: a restart must never double-submit or strand a stack. This is the reliability-fallback backbone. Split out of S5 (`perpetual-ultrasonic-raccoon`).

## Files
- `infra/autolander/loop.py` ‚Äî EDIT: heartbeat write, emergency-stop sentinel check, SIGTERM drain + recovery-record write, restart-recovery reconciliation.
- `infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander` ‚Äî EDIT: the `HEALTHCHECK`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **Heartbeat (AC5):** the bot writes a timestamp file in the state volume once per its 15 s poll (S2 cadence); its age is served as `heartbeat_age_s` by S5c's status endpoint. Contract: freshness ‚â§ 15 s; `land`'s `lander_down` treats age **> 90 s** (6√ó poll) as down.
- [ ] **Liveness/restart (AC4):** a `HEALTHCHECK` marks the container `unhealthy` on a **hang** by stat-ing the **local heartbeat file** (unhealthy if older than the staleness threshold) ‚Äî it MUST NOT probe Gerrit (a Gerrit outage would flap restarts). The `willfarrell/autoheal` sidecar (S5a) restarts the `autoheal=true`-labeled container on `unhealthy`.
- [ ] **Emergency-stop (prior art: Zuul `pause`):** an emergency-stop **sentinel file** in the state volume (presence = stop; operator creates to pause, removes to resume) transitions the bot to `paused` (the S2 `phase` value) ‚Äî STOPS consuming new `Autosubmit` changes. Lives on the state volume so a redeploy does not silently un-pause.
- [ ] **Drain-on-shutdown + crash-safe recovery (RELIABILITY FALLBACK):** on SIGTERM the bot finishes the in-flight `wipChain`, writes its outcome to S3's marker, then exits (prior art: Zuul `graceful`); the drain is **time-bounded** (SIGTERM deadline ~ the CI-wait timeout) ‚Äî past the deadline it writes a **concrete recovery record** (`recovery.json` in the state volume: the `wipChain` snapshot ‚Äî `change_id`, `chain_member_ids`, `tested_shas`, `phase`, `re_drive_count`, and the handback `acknowledged` flag) and exits rather than blocking the redeploy. **On restart** the bot reads `recovery.json` + reconciles against LIVE Gerrit and S3's markers BEFORE selecting new work: a stack that was mid-submit is checked against Gerrit (already merged? then ack; not merged? re-drive); a mid-handback stack completes the idempotent `Autosubmit`-removal (keyed by S3's `acknowledged=false`). No double-submit; no stranded stack.
- [ ] **Crash-recovery/idempotency test (the reliability guarantee):** an automated test kills the bot at each phase (`rebasing`, `awaiting_verified`, `submitting`, mid-handback) and asserts the restarted instance neither double-submits nor strands a stack, and converges the S3 marker to `acknowledged=true`.
data.title: S5d Liveness/heartbeat/autoheal + emergency-stop + drain + crash-safe recovery
